Kaylin Holden.review_text: She is definitely making Business Communication far more complicated. This was an introduction class, but the grading, and overall course structure makes it one of the hardest classes I have taken as a business major. I recommend being careful enrolling to this class.

She is taking an intro BCOM class WAY too seriously. Her grading is very harsh and is honestly just trying to complicate the course. I've heard a lot about other BCOM professors being much more relaxed. I wouldn't recommend unless you're ready to be graded by a professor on a business communication power trip. Also does NOT round up grades.

Pretty easy class, Kaylins instructions were clear and effective but she was very particular about how she wanted things written so you had to follow specific layouts. Overall an easy class with little workload.

Cheryl Brodersen.review_text: The BCOM 214 Program at U of A needs some serious reworking, but Cheryl is one of the better options for who to take. She's willing to work with her students while also giving you ample opportunities to really get good feedback (if you go talk to her). She often lets out class early and has funny stories she's willing to share. Definately take her!

One of the best business professors I've taken so far. She is very open with grading, assignments are not that difficult, and will round up your grades without having to ask. Super helpful for getting into Eller and other classes, would love to have her for more classes.

Dr. B is honestly amazing. It's an easy class if you simply focus during the first 2–3 weeks, since the writing structure stays consistent throughout. She's fun and really tries to make the class enjoyable. If you attend class and visit her office hours before assignments are due, there's no reason not to enjoy this course and also get an A.
Grading is very slow. Attendance is all of your grade.
Dr. B is the best. She is so sweet caring and flexible. Go to all of her classes and it is an easy A in the course.
Great Teacher! little points in the class so it is easy to get a B if you don't try.

She cares a little about class, but over spent more time talking about herself, family and her MBA students she teaches than ever actually explaining anything. Do whatever you can do to not buy the book, as the school and the BCOM teachers have collusion going that force you to purchase materials written by the professor. Super unethical.

BCOM214 sucks but you have to take it, so take Cheryl, she's the only professor who is aware of the issues in the course and cares enough to help students who get screwed. There is a disconnect between what is taught vs grade expectations but she will work with you. D2L lecture videos are absolutely torturous to sit through - you have been warned

This course showed me how to write business professionally and this teacher made it very clear and to the point. The workload is not a lot and if you just go to class you will easily get an A. Great teacher!

Dr. B is AMAZING!! If you have the chance to take her class, DO IT. You will learn, you will pass, and you will feel appreciated and cared for. I have no bad words for Dr. B, this class is my favorite class from all I have had in a long time. Definitely recommend Dr. B!!

Dr. B was absolutely incredible and so much fun in class! She was always chill and willing to work with students, she truly wants to see everyone succeed. She's great at teaching the content in a way that everyone can grasp and makes it fun to engage with. If you have to take this class go with Dr. B you won't regret it!!

Definitely take Dr. B if you have the chance!! I was considering taking the online section, but Dr. B's in person class was so fun and engaging. Attendance is technically optional but you do get points for showing up (APs). Every assignment is simple and she gives time to work on it in class. I wish she taught upper division classes!!!

This might've been the easiest course I've taken in college. Dr. Brodersen is incredibly friendly and caring. The lessons are very straightforward with what she wants you to know and I completed most of the assignments in 10 minutes max. She gives good feedback and will let you revise anything. I came to class just to talk to her, she's so nice.

Cheryl is the nicest teacher that I have had at this school. She is extremely caring for her students and rewards her students for coming to class. She is super funny and wants her students to succeed!

Cheryl is the best! Such a genuine heart for her students and she is hilarious as well. If you go to class you will do well. She is a fair grader and tells you exactly what she wants to see in your writing. The classes are clear and structured, 100% recommend this professor.

She is one of the best professors I have had at UofA so far. She genuinely cares and teaches you how to be successful in life with the material she teaches rather than for a grade. She doesn't really ever give anything lower than an 80 on anything and was so entertaining that going to class was fun and worth it.

Dr.Broderson really cares about her students. She will do everything she can to teach you how to be good at communicating. Gives great feedback and great grading criteria. Really wants to see you succeed. She is a great person to work with. Highly recommend taking her for your BCOM class.

Dr. B was the best professor I have had in college. Not only is she a good teacher, she is so nice and understanding if you need an extension due to life difficulties. She is clear and concise in her lectures and is more than happy to work with you through assignments. She is helpful in office hours and wants her students to succeed.

I LOVE DR. B. She is so funny and is literally the sweetest teacher ever. She's very open to communication and questions. Also very understandable if you miss class for a personal reason. Best professor I've had at the UA by far. If you ask her to look over your homework before you turn it in or "fishbowl" it, you get an A. Would def take again.

Professor Brod is my favorite. So easygoing and knows we are college students. Always cracks jokes in class, grades very fair, and accommodates valid excuses and problems that pop up during the year. TAKE HER CLASS!

Dr. B is a great professor! She cracks jokes and is extremely easygoing. You get a lot of time to work on assignments, and she is an easy grader. While attendance is required, she is a very understanding professor and will accept your absence sometimes without question. Deductions for lateness are also hardly applied.

Cheryl is a gem! She gives you a lot of time to work on assignments in class. Her assignment instructions are super clear, and she goes over examples so it's easy to get a good grade as long as you try. Attendance isn't really mandatory but you'll do much better if you go to class. Ends class early sometimes and doesn't make us go on Fridays.

Cheryl was an amazing professor. She really cares not only about the grade you are receiving but if you understand the material. She did a great job adapting to zoom and cared about how we were adjusting. All around good person:)

Cheryl is a great teacher. Lectures are clear and explain the homework assignment for the week. She has classes were she edits students homework so the class knows what an A looks like, but is also super helpful with it in office hours. Theres one group project, but overall easy class
She's a very great professor. She gets off-topic at times but she's very caring and funny. She's there for you if you need the extra support. I would 100% recommend her class.
Cheryl is hilarious and caring, BCOM is an easy course and she makes it worth going to the lectures
You have to take BCOM so when you do try and get Cheryl. She is one of the best BCOM teachers and actually wants you to succeed. She will give you feedback and make class fun.

Not a good professor who always talk about some other things that are not related to the class materials in class. She is a hard grader. No matter how well you write your paper, she will not give you more than 80.

Tons of quizzes - she is awesome though. makes class basic and easy. The stuff you learn is cool, but super easy. She is sympathetic, will help where and when she can. She's super laid back and easy to deal with. She's the best professor affiliated with the bcom content, all the rest I hear horror stories about.

Professor seems like such a great person, but there seems to be a bit of a fundamental disconnect in what's taught, vs. what is supposed to be learned. She's definitely a great person for feedback, but better instruction is a necessity.

She is a great person, but no so much of a professor. She barely takes time to go over the material and then gives out C's for no good reason. We will do examples of homework in class and she tells us this is what we should do and then takes off points for doing it. The class content itself is cake. Losing my 4.0 to this mad woman.

Catherine Jennings.review_text: Insanely easy IF YOU SHOW UP TO CLASS She tries to make it so no work ever goes home, and you do the assignments as a class. You submit the assignments, and get checked in class meaning you walk out of the classroom never having to care about the course. I had around 5 absences which after communicating through email, she walked me through the work

I liked Jennings as a professor but the class felt very disorganized. Super easy work if you follow along with her, so GO TO CLASS! The chances of her having the directions or any way to get into contact with her is unlikely. I got a B when I very much deserved an A. She also told me I would keep an A even with missed one assignment... she lied.

BCOM214 is a lot easier than other classes Jennings teaches. This class is very easy, with little to no homework, as the assignments can easily be completed in class if you follow along with the prof.
We was a great lecturer who made everything simple and cared about her students.

She was super laid back and understanding. Class was beneficial and she didn't grade harshly. As long as you do what she tells you and turn the assignments in you will get an A. Instructions were unclear sometimes, and d2l was a little messy, but she cleared everything up quickly. Didn't update the grade book often. Other than that I recommend!
Understanding, caring, and easy class. Top professor at this school
Catherine is amazing! She is super relaxed and chill. She waived off our midterms and finals. I freaking love her. Easy A!

Dr. Jennings is very knowledgeable, and really funny. But, she is very relaxed and often cancelled class and gives you a lot of wiggle room. She graded most assignments the week before grades were due. Overall, this is an easy class but I don't feel like I learned much.

Marisa Michaels.review_text: Just don't take her class. She always gives the feedback AFTER our assignments were graded. And she and the TAs ARE REALLY HARD GRADERS!!!!!! And the Lockdown Browser really SUCK!!!!! It even can't autosave!!! DON'T TAKE THIS CLASS EVER!!!!

If you can avoid her as a teacher do!! She is one of the toughest graders and gives you zero feedback on drafts until they are already submitted. She will find anything and everything to mark you for and makes it incredibly hard to get good grades. She also never really teaches in class so you are left in the dark on what to do and how to succeed.

This class was not for me. If you like getting no feedback and being graded harshly this is the class for you. She'll take points off for things as little as commas. She doesn't give direction and grades too hard for a simple writing class that is mandatory. She also can come across as rude at times so for your sake PLEASE spare yourself!
I love Marisa, she is the best!

She explains the assignments in class so as long as you're paying attention you should be good. She also does peer review grading in class and gives you a good idea of what she is looking for. She says to get the book, but she posts enough resources that it wasn't necessary. Be prepared to write a lot. There is a paper due almost every week.
Marisa is a very nice teacher! Although BCOM214 is not a easy class, she tried her best to help students.

BCOM 214 is a difficult class but Marisa makes the class simple and easy to understand. She sets up her class so you only need to show up once a week. She is also very helpful with giving feedback as long as you seek her out. Definitely recommend Marisa if there is space in her class. Assignments are difficult but you will learn a lot in this class

There are a lot of written assignments, basically one due every week. She is very very picky and critical, but gives amazing feedback so you will learn a lot from her. She splits the class up so you occasionally only have it once a week. But i stress the importance of attending feedback fridays. She has a good personality is enjoyable to listen to.

She is very knowledgeable about business writing and is very kind in her feedback, but is very willing to help students who are struggling with the assignments. This class is harder than expected and the writing assignments take more time and effort than expected because the grading is pretty tough, but just try your best and you should be good.

Marisa is an awesome professor! I took this class as an elective going into Eller, and I highly reccommend! Marisa is hilarious and keeps the material interesting! A pretty easy class, and it was so much fun to go to class because of Marisa's personality!

James Rashan.review_text: Professor Rashan is the best teacher I have had. He is incredibly thoughtful and understanding. His class was very beneficial and I learned a lot through attending class and completing assignments. I had a personal issue during the semester and professor Rashan was very accessible outside of class and helped me get back on track. I highly recommend

He is a very good professor, albeit slightly more picky than some other professors when grading assignments. He is also very easy to talk to if you're stuck, and has a great sense of humor and energy in the class. Even though he's picky with grading, he's not a hard grader at all as long as you hit every point on the rubric.

Mr Rashan was my favorite professor this semester. He sincerly cared about his class and wanted us to do our best. He taugh us the improtance of working with new people every class period. He gives the vibes of a cool uncle that just wants the best for you.

John Delaney.review_text: He was a really nice guy, except extremely inconsistent in grading. The assignments given should be easy however, he docks points off on everything. He is extremely vague and his lectures do not relate to assignments or tests.

John is very kind, but his class can be tough. He's a strict grader, and I had to do weekly Zoom meetings with him just to get close to perfect scores. Attendance is mandatory with only three allowed absences. He sometimes struggles to communicate clearly in lectures, which is hard to follow. I'd suggest taking a different BCOM professor.

Carletta Sanders-Chavers.review_text: Sanders-Chavers is a tough grader, but so long as you ask every question you could possibly have about what she's looking for in the assignment and its formatting, you'll get a good grade. While attendance is mandatory, she is understanding if you have a valid reason and provide appropriate notice. Be very detail-oriented for the assignments.

Charles Waldburger.review_text: He is my favorite professor I have gotten at U of A! Seriously blessed to have been put in his course! He has made everything so flexible whether it be extending deadlines for the benefit of the class, responding to emails almost immediately, or allowing us to choose in person or on zoom! Seriously amazing I couldn't say better about him!

Consider yourself lucky if you get Professor Waldburger for BCOM 214. He is super sweet and caring and is such an easy grader. He makes his expectations very clear, so if you follow his instructions you should get close to full credit on each assignment. He's a great guy!

Caitlin Hills.review_text: Too subjective as a grader. Refuses to provide constructive feedback and becomes hostile if you ask her to elaborate on grades. You can be an honors English student, and she will still find something to nitpick rather than taking the entire assignment into consideration.
Extremely professional and very fast grader. Always responded within 24 hours. I learned a great deal from this course.
The single worst professor I've ever had. Unclear instructions and grades harshly for no apparent reason. When asked about very questionable grading she has no response just ignores it.

Aimee Mcfarland.review_text: Despite being a Business communications class, Aimee is horrible in communicating with students about course assignments. What she wants in the assignment is different from the assignment template and guide videos on D2l. Though changed now, we were even strictly graded on the draft for their assignments. Take teacher who appear on guide video!!

This is the worst professor I have had at the UofA. Calling her a teacher is a stretch because she does not teach, yet she expects you to produce assignments that meet her expectations. She never made her expectations clear.  Class time was wasted. I can't believe I actually paid money  to take a class from her.
McFarland is one of the worst professors I've had a UA. She is unhelpful and honestly a bad communicator, so I have no idea what she's doing teaching Business Communication.

Professor McFarland was fairly unhelpful. She did not give adequate feedback on assignments and changed her grading style, as well as instructions throughout the course. The class times were not very productive, and I did not feel as if I learned much from her teaching. For a business communications class she did not communicate well at all.

Aimee's class is great from a foundational perspective - it may seem like common sense, but it's surprising how bad basic grammar conventions can be for a cohort of native English speakers and writers. She's a very fair and straightforward grader. Her basics in parallelism, BLOT, and WIIFY spare us from years of writing cringey professional emails.

Have you ever wanted a B in a class? Guess what, this is the class for you because you surely aren't receiving an A! Don't believe me, take this class with her and find out for yourself! One of the WORST professors at UofA and highly recommend taking a different teacher! If your a 4.0 student, Congratulations, you are now a 3.8 student:)

ATTENTION! You will get a B in this class! If you are a 4.0 student think again before taking this class because you surely will not have one after taking this class. Although it is potentially possible to receive an A with this teacher, the overwhelming majority of students taking this course will receive a B. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS with her!

Idk why Aimee has such a low rating. This class was so easy if you just followed the outlines she gave you and paid attention in the lecture you're supposed to attend. She goes over almost every written assignment and shows you exactly how it should look and what to write. This class was not hard.

One of the worst teachers. This class is so easy and helpful with Amber or any other teacher. VERY harsh grader but doesn't give feedback, is inconsistent and disrespectful. Hard to stay awake in class. Not helpful when you ask a question she just says "why don't you tell me" at anything. Takes long to grade. DO NOT TAKE!!!!!!

She is one of the worst professors ive ever had at the UofA. Highly recommended not taking her class and finding a different teacher. She is a very poor communicator and does not treat students well. She is very unprofessional and said that it was frustrating to have to answer my email.

Professor McFarland is a great teacher. Her lectures prepare you for assignments, as she gives examples of them, so attend class. Take advantage of fishbowls as she will tell you what to correct. If you aren't sure about anything reach out to her or attend office hours. She is so helpful and wants you to succeed. Follow the rubrics for good grades!

Aimee is a great teacher and incredibly understanding. Her work is laid out so easily and as long as you follow directions you will get the grade you want. I don't know how she can make her class any easier. Make sure you do what she asks and you will get an A. Definitely recommend!

Aimee is a good professor, she does a good job of explaining the content. However, to achieve a good grade in this class, you are going to have to do way more work than people who take BCOM with another professor. No extra credit opps and a strict class setting. BCOM is already a mindnumbing class, at least take it with a fun professor.

She is a solid professor. Nice lady, very willing to help students. My biggest issue was that her assignment criteria was kind of unclear and she was a tough grader and kind of nit-picky. Other than that I recommend her for this course.

Aimee was one of the best professors I've had at the U of A! Attendance is mandatory but you are able to miss a certain amount of classes without penalty. 

I  HIGHLY recommend attending office hours to have her review your work and guarantee yourself an A.

She is so amazing!! I took her last semester for BCOM 214, and even though I changed my major from business I still did not drop her class because she is the most understanding and helpful professor I have had at the University of Arizona. She always gives great feedback and is extremely understanding. Love her!!

I took Aimee's class a year ago and had the chance to use what I learned during my summer internship. Its easy to get an A in her class if you do what your supposed to and read feedback. I don't understand the negative ratings except that she expects students to learn in her class. I pay for school myself so I'm glad she made it worth my money.

Aimee is amazing, I really felt her course content was relevant and she was right there anytime anyone had questions. I always knew exactly what to expect from the assignments and exams. I wish she taught all my classes. :)

Aimee is the most empathetic professor I have ever had, she cares so much about her students and wants them to succeed. You will do well in her class if you attend class as much as you can and do your work! I highly recommend taking her course.

Great professor. Compared my friends BCOM professors and they had more steps to assignments while we did not! I am glad I took her class because she is such a caring person and will always look out for you!

I don't understand the bad reviews for Aimee she is such a caring person and will always be there to help with either personal or academically situations. Her office hours were super useful. The assignments weren't tough because in class she would go over it. For exams, the study guides were helpful because it'd give you a good grasp on the topics.

Puts students and their mental health first; very approachable and makes herself available. Just come to most classes and you will know how to get an A on every assignment, and if you are confused she's always willing to help or review your work with you via email or during office hours.

Aimee is a good person. I don't think she realizes how means she's being, but she also means well. BCOM214 is a waste of a class, but that isn't her fault. She is a tough grader and will make you work for your grade, but an A is very possible. She is someone who is there for you as a student, which definitely made me feel better.

Aimee is very sweet and her class is very passable if you go to office hours. She is very willing to help and wants you to learn the material. Grading criteria can still be confusing and it's not clear what you lose points on. Try and get things done early and get feedback before you turn it in.

I'll start off by saying I do not understand all the poor ratings for this professor. She is super friendly and approachable and fair with grading. The trick to succeeding in her class is to take good notes on formatting she uses for her writing assignments. I did this every time and got all but one A on an assignment, and I am not a strong writer.

She kind of annoyed me. Her lectures weren't very helpful for assignments- everyone was always really confused on what we were supposed to be doing. The instructions were also super vague. She also took random points away from assignments w/ little to no explanation. Shes nice, just not the best professor in the world

Aimee is a great professor and truly wants her students to succeed. If you pay attention when she fishbowls and points out mistakes, assignments will be very easy for you. There were a lot of assignments but shouldn't take you more than 2 hours. Very understanding. If you're going to miss class let her know and she won't count it as an absence.
Lectures are not very useful in doing projects and lack important content. Do not feel to learn a lot from this class from a lot of homework. Also hard to get a good grade. Boring class overall.

Aimee has been so understanding this semester! She extends most deadlines if you ask and fishbowls a lot of assignments during class. She is very helpful with feedback. There are a lot of assignments, and most of them are easy. Attendance is mandatory, but class is interesting and worthwhile to earn feedback.

Aimee deserves a 5/5. She has been very understanding during covid and has extended deadlines multiple times so we could get extra feedback during class before submitting. There are many assignments but they aren't extremely difficult if you ask questions and some are just note taking assignments-super easy. She makes class enjoyable! Take her

Professor Mcfarland cares a lot about her students and is always willing to extend extra help if you need it. Her assignments require following directions closely and you have to work hard, but if you do you will be successful. I would recommend asking lots of questions and making sure to stay on top of assignments.
please save yourself the pain and take this class with a different professor. She is a terrible teacher

If you want to get an A in BCOM214, don't take this class. If you have a choice, Do Not pick this teacher you will work 10 times as hard for projects ad papers while getting a lower score at every possible point. She says that she is preparing you for the "real" world, well this ain't the real world, this is college. we are trying to learn.

I read the ratings here and got nervous when it came time to choose p/f because of the comments. I didn't think I would do well on the research report. I ended up getting an A and would have gotten an A in the class. Aimee told me I would likely do well, but I didnt believe her. Now I regret it.

Aimee is an incredible woman! She is sweet, caring, & enjoys her job. However, she is a very harsh grader w/ unclear feedback & difficult-to-understand instructions. I really think she'd be a much better counselor or therapist than a BCOM professor. Possible to do work last minute and still get by. I selected the Pass/Fail bc GPA would def tank.

Funny how people who don't do well on assignments vent on Coursicle. McFarland is a tough grader but she gives feedback to tell you what you did wrong and makes herself very available. I want to be prepared for writing and my classes at Eller and that's what she does.

Professor McFarland seems like a great person, but she is a really harsh grader. You really have to stay on your A game the entire semester to even barely get an A. This makes it hard when you're learning new information because it gives you no room to make any mistakes. Definitely go to her office hours though!
Incredibly rude to students and super hard grader for no reason. Passive aggressive towards any questions and gives no guideline on how to do assignments. Take with another professor if possible.

Continuing the trend of substandard ratings, let me add my two cents. What a terrible experience with this Professor. Inconsistent grading and lack of assignment clarity were my main points of frustration. Find an alternative route at Eller

Aimee cares about her students. Not only does she want them to do well in her class, she also wants them to be successful in the real world. She's a tough grader, but think of it as tough love. Whether it's reading extra material or seeking for help during office hours, this class requires students to actively participate and put in the effort.

Aimee is a teacher that cares about her subject matter and holds her students to a high standard. Attendance is mandatory and due dates are rigid. As other posts have mentioned, understanding what is expected in a given assignment was tough at times. Visit her office hours and make sure she knows you and you'll be just fine in the class.

The worst teacher at Eller. She sucks. Avoid this professor at all costs. I was forced to take it with her. She is the toughest grader ever. Her lectures were of no help. She is mean to all of her students. You do all of these pointless assignments that you will never use in the real world.
I was afraid of taking this class but I have learned so much. I am doing an internship over the summer and feel confident that I will be able to communicate effectively.
Definitely take her. I learned so much.

Definitely knows her stuff. I thought she was a harsh grader, although she did give clear grading criteria for every assignment. After getting through my first semester at Eller, though, I realize why she had the expectations she did. I was prepared and did very well. Thanks for getting me ready for Eller!

McFarland was definitely tough, but she got me prepared for Eller and the professional world. A boss would expect me to perform well, and she is no different. While it was tough throughout the semester, I know I learned a lot.

It ain't high school no more. Read instructions! Turn in work on time! Go to office hours! Ask Q's if unclear on an asgmt! Take responsibility for the grades received. If u can't put in effort now, u won't even be able to experience how challenging Eller gets (bc u have to pass class first, u know). She's tough, but that just means work harder.

Aimee's class/assignments are difficult at times (her standards are high), but she is willing to work with students and meet outside of class. Meeting with her in office hours helps tremendously because she explains things; however, it's up to you to put in the work, effort, and show up to class.
Assignments are graded tough, be sure to follow all directions. Overall, the class was fine.

Erin Paradis.review_text: You have to take BCOM 214 and from what I've heard, Paradis is probably the best professor to take it with. Regardless, you have to put in the work in this class. 1 assignment a week on avg. She's nice and emails back quickly. Glad I had her compared to the stories I've heard about the other professors. Paradis takes attendance everyday too so :(

Erin was an amazing professor and made each class fun. The assignment are not that hard and the tests are easy if you regularly attend class. Erin gives great feedback and I looked forward to going to this class each week. I would 10/10 recommend taking BCOM with Erin.

all_reviews.review_text: She is definitely making Business Communication far more complicated. This was an introduction class, but the grading, and overall course structure makes it one of the hardest classes I have taken as a business major. I recommend being careful enrolling to this class.

She is taking an intro BCOM class WAY too seriously. Her grading is very harsh and is honestly just trying to complicate the course. I've heard a lot about other BCOM professors being much more relaxed. I wouldn't recommend unless you're ready to be graded by a professor on a business communication power trip. Also does NOT round up grades.

Pretty easy class, Kaylins instructions were clear and effective but she was very particular about how she wanted things written so you had to follow specific layouts. Overall an easy class with little workload.

The BCOM 214 Program at U of A needs some serious reworking, but Cheryl is one of the better options for who to take. She's willing to work with her students while also giving you ample opportunities to really get good feedback (if you go talk to her). She often lets out class early and has funny stories she's willing to share. Definately take her!

One of the best business professors I've taken so far. She is very open with grading, assignments are not that difficult, and will round up your grades without having to ask. Super helpful for getting into Eller and other classes, would love to have her for more classes.

Dr. B is honestly amazing. It's an easy class if you simply focus during the first 2–3 weeks, since the writing structure stays consistent throughout. She's fun and really tries to make the class enjoyable. If you attend class and visit her office hours before assignments are due, there's no reason not to enjoy this course and also get an A.
Grading is very slow. Attendance is all of your grade.
Dr. B is the best. She is so sweet caring and flexible. Go to all of her classes and it is an easy A in the course.
Great Teacher! little points in the class so it is easy to get a B if you don't try.

She cares a little about class, but over spent more time talking about herself, family and her MBA students she teaches than ever actually explaining anything. Do whatever you can do to not buy the book, as the school and the BCOM teachers have collusion going that force you to purchase materials written by the professor. Super unethical.

BCOM214 sucks but you have to take it, so take Cheryl, she's the only professor who is aware of the issues in the course and cares enough to help students who get screwed. There is a disconnect between what is taught vs grade expectations but she will work with you. D2L lecture videos are absolutely torturous to sit through - you have been warned

This course showed me how to write business professionally and this teacher made it very clear and to the point. The workload is not a lot and if you just go to class you will easily get an A. Great teacher!

Dr. B is AMAZING!! If you have the chance to take her class, DO IT. You will learn, you will pass, and you will feel appreciated and cared for. I have no bad words for Dr. B, this class is my favorite class from all I have had in a long time. Definitely recommend Dr. B!!

Dr. B was absolutely incredible and so much fun in class! She was always chill and willing to work with students, she truly wants to see everyone succeed. She's great at teaching the content in a way that everyone can grasp and makes it fun to engage with. If you have to take this class go with Dr. B you won't regret it!!

Definitely take Dr. B if you have the chance!! I was considering taking the online section, but Dr. B's in person class was so fun and engaging. Attendance is technically optional but you do get points for showing up (APs). Every assignment is simple and she gives time to work on it in class. I wish she taught upper division classes!!!

This might've been the easiest course I've taken in college. Dr. Brodersen is incredibly friendly and caring. The lessons are very straightforward with what she wants you to know and I completed most of the assignments in 10 minutes max. She gives good feedback and will let you revise anything. I came to class just to talk to her, she's so nice.

Cheryl is the nicest teacher that I have had at this school. She is extremely caring for her students and rewards her students for coming to class. She is super funny and wants her students to succeed!

Cheryl is the best! Such a genuine heart for her students and she is hilarious as well. If you go to class you will do well. She is a fair grader and tells you exactly what she wants to see in your writing. The classes are clear and structured, 100% recommend this professor.

She is one of the best professors I have had at UofA so far. She genuinely cares and teaches you how to be successful in life with the material she teaches rather than for a grade. She doesn't really ever give anything lower than an 80 on anything and was so entertaining that going to class was fun and worth it.

Dr.Broderson really cares about her students. She will do everything she can to teach you how to be good at communicating. Gives great feedback and great grading criteria. Really wants to see you succeed. She is a great person to work with. Highly recommend taking her for your BCOM class.

Dr. B was the best professor I have had in college. Not only is she a good teacher, she is so nice and understanding if you need an extension due to life difficulties. She is clear and concise in her lectures and is more than happy to work with you through assignments. She is helpful in office hours and wants her students to succeed.

I LOVE DR. B. She is so funny and is literally the sweetest teacher ever. She's very open to communication and questions. Also very understandable if you miss class for a personal reason. Best professor I've had at the UA by far. If you ask her to look over your homework before you turn it in or "fishbowl" it, you get an A. Would def take again.

Professor Brod is my favorite. So easygoing and knows we are college students. Always cracks jokes in class, grades very fair, and accommodates valid excuses and problems that pop up during the year. TAKE HER CLASS!

Dr. B is a great professor! She cracks jokes and is extremely easygoing. You get a lot of time to work on assignments, and she is an easy grader. While attendance is required, she is a very understanding professor and will accept your absence sometimes without question. Deductions for lateness are also hardly applied.

Cheryl is a gem! She gives you a lot of time to work on assignments in class. Her assignment instructions are super clear, and she goes over examples so it's easy to get a good grade as long as you try. Attendance isn't really mandatory but you'll do much better if you go to class. Ends class early sometimes and doesn't make us go on Fridays.

Cheryl was an amazing professor. She really cares not only about the grade you are receiving but if you understand the material. She did a great job adapting to zoom and cared about how we were adjusting. All around good person:)

Cheryl is a great teacher. Lectures are clear and explain the homework assignment for the week. She has classes were she edits students homework so the class knows what an A looks like, but is also super helpful with it in office hours. Theres one group project, but overall easy class
She's a very great professor. She gets off-topic at times but she's very caring and funny. She's there for you if you need the extra support. I would 100% recommend her class.
Cheryl is hilarious and caring, BCOM is an easy course and she makes it worth going to the lectures
You have to take BCOM so when you do try and get Cheryl. She is one of the best BCOM teachers and actually wants you to succeed. She will give you feedback and make class fun.

Not a good professor who always talk about some other things that are not related to the class materials in class. She is a hard grader. No matter how well you write your paper, she will not give you more than 80.

Tons of quizzes - she is awesome though. makes class basic and easy. The stuff you learn is cool, but super easy. She is sympathetic, will help where and when she can. She's super laid back and easy to deal with. She's the best professor affiliated with the bcom content, all the rest I hear horror stories about.

Professor seems like such a great person, but there seems to be a bit of a fundamental disconnect in what's taught, vs. what is supposed to be learned. She's definitely a great person for feedback, but better instruction is a necessity.

She is a great person, but no so much of a professor. She barely takes time to go over the material and then gives out C's for no good reason. We will do examples of homework in class and she tells us this is what we should do and then takes off points for doing it. The class content itself is cake. Losing my 4.0 to this mad woman.

Insanely easy IF YOU SHOW UP TO CLASS She tries to make it so no work ever goes home, and you do the assignments as a class. You submit the assignments, and get checked in class meaning you walk out of the classroom never having to care about the course. I had around 5 absences which after communicating through email, she walked me through the work

I liked Jennings as a professor but the class felt very disorganized. Super easy work if you follow along with her, so GO TO CLASS! The chances of her having the directions or any way to get into contact with her is unlikely. I got a B when I very much deserved an A. She also told me I would keep an A even with missed one assignment... she lied.

BCOM214 is a lot easier than other classes Jennings teaches. This class is very easy, with little to no homework, as the assignments can easily be completed in class if you follow along with the prof.
We was a great lecturer who made everything simple and cared about her students.

She was super laid back and understanding. Class was beneficial and she didn't grade harshly. As long as you do what she tells you and turn the assignments in you will get an A. Instructions were unclear sometimes, and d2l was a little messy, but she cleared everything up quickly. Didn't update the grade book often. Other than that I recommend!
Understanding, caring, and easy class. Top professor at this school
Catherine is amazing! She is super relaxed and chill. She waived off our midterms and finals. I freaking love her. Easy A!

Dr. Jennings is very knowledgeable, and really funny. But, she is very relaxed and often cancelled class and gives you a lot of wiggle room. She graded most assignments the week before grades were due. Overall, this is an easy class but I don't feel like I learned much.

Just don't take her class. She always gives the feedback AFTER our assignments were graded. And she and the TAs ARE REALLY HARD GRADERS!!!!!! And the Lockdown Browser really SUCK!!!!! It even can't autosave!!! DON'T TAKE THIS CLASS EVER!!!!

If you can avoid her as a teacher do!! She is one of the toughest graders and gives you zero feedback on drafts until they are already submitted. She will find anything and everything to mark you for and makes it incredibly hard to get good grades. She also never really teaches in class so you are left in the dark on what to do and how to succeed.

This class was not for me. If you like getting no feedback and being graded harshly this is the class for you. She'll take points off for things as little as commas. She doesn't give direction and grades too hard for a simple writing class that is mandatory. She also can come across as rude at times so for your sake PLEASE spare yourself!
I love Marisa, she is the best!

She explains the assignments in class so as long as you're paying attention you should be good. She also does peer review grading in class and gives you a good idea of what she is looking for. She says to get the book, but she posts enough resources that it wasn't necessary. Be prepared to write a lot. There is a paper due almost every week.
Marisa is a very nice teacher! Although BCOM214 is not a easy class, she tried her best to help students.

BCOM 214 is a difficult class but Marisa makes the class simple and easy to understand. She sets up her class so you only need to show up once a week. She is also very helpful with giving feedback as long as you seek her out. Definitely recommend Marisa if there is space in her class. Assignments are difficult but you will learn a lot in this class

There are a lot of written assignments, basically one due every week. She is very very picky and critical, but gives amazing feedback so you will learn a lot from her. She splits the class up so you occasionally only have it once a week. But i stress the importance of attending feedback fridays. She has a good personality is enjoyable to listen to.

She is very knowledgeable about business writing and is very kind in her feedback, but is very willing to help students who are struggling with the assignments. This class is harder than expected and the writing assignments take more time and effort than expected because the grading is pretty tough, but just try your best and you should be good.

Marisa is an awesome professor! I took this class as an elective going into Eller, and I highly reccommend! Marisa is hilarious and keeps the material interesting! A pretty easy class, and it was so much fun to go to class because of Marisa's personality!

Professor Rashan is the best teacher I have had. He is incredibly thoughtful and understanding. His class was very beneficial and I learned a lot through attending class and completing assignments. I had a personal issue during the semester and professor Rashan was very accessible outside of class and helped me get back on track. I highly recommend

He is a very good professor, albeit slightly more picky than some other professors when grading assignments. He is also very easy to talk to if you're stuck, and has a great sense of humor and energy in the class. Even though he's picky with grading, he's not a hard grader at all as long as you hit every point on the rubric.

Mr Rashan was my favorite professor this semester. He sincerly cared about his class and wanted us to do our best. He taugh us the improtance of working with new people every class period. He gives the vibes of a cool uncle that just wants the best for you.

He was a really nice guy, except extremely inconsistent in grading. The assignments given should be easy however, he docks points off on everything. He is extremely vague and his lectures do not relate to assignments or tests.

John is very kind, but his class can be tough. He's a strict grader, and I had to do weekly Zoom meetings with him just to get close to perfect scores. Attendance is mandatory with only three allowed absences. He sometimes struggles to communicate clearly in lectures, which is hard to follow. I'd suggest taking a different BCOM professor.

Sanders-Chavers is a tough grader, but so long as you ask every question you could possibly have about what she's looking for in the assignment and its formatting, you'll get a good grade. While attendance is mandatory, she is understanding if you have a valid reason and provide appropriate notice. Be very detail-oriented for the assignments.

He is my favorite professor I have gotten at U of A! Seriously blessed to have been put in his course! He has made everything so flexible whether it be extending deadlines for the benefit of the class, responding to emails almost immediately, or allowing us to choose in person or on zoom! Seriously amazing I couldn't say better about him!

Consider yourself lucky if you get Professor Waldburger for BCOM 214. He is super sweet and caring and is such an easy grader. He makes his expectations very clear, so if you follow his instructions you should get close to full credit on each assignment. He's a great guy!

Too subjective as a grader. Refuses to provide constructive feedback and becomes hostile if you ask her to elaborate on grades. You can be an honors English student, and she will still find something to nitpick rather than taking the entire assignment into consideration.
Best teacher in elller
Extremely professional and very fast grader. Always responded within 24 hours. I learned a great deal from this course.
The single worst professor I've ever had. Unclear instructions and grades harshly for no apparent reason. When asked about very questionable grading she has no response just ignores it.

Despite being a Business communications class, Aimee is horrible in communicating with students about course assignments. What she wants in the assignment is different from the assignment template and guide videos on D2l. Though changed now, we were even strictly graded on the draft for their assignments. Take teacher who appear on guide video!!

This is the worst professor I have had at the UofA. Calling her a teacher is a stretch because she does not teach, yet she expects you to produce assignments that meet her expectations. She never made her expectations clear.  Class time was wasted. I can't believe I actually paid money  to take a class from her.
McFarland is one of the worst professors I've had a UA. She is unhelpful and honestly a bad communicator, so I have no idea what she's doing teaching Business Communication.

Professor McFarland was fairly unhelpful. She did not give adequate feedback on assignments and changed her grading style, as well as instructions throughout the course. The class times were not very productive, and I did not feel as if I learned much from her teaching. For a business communications class she did not communicate well at all.

Aimee's class is great from a foundational perspective - it may seem like common sense, but it's surprising how bad basic grammar conventions can be for a cohort of native English speakers and writers. She's a very fair and straightforward grader. Her basics in parallelism, BLOT, and WIIFY spare us from years of writing cringey professional emails.

Have you ever wanted a B in a class? Guess what, this is the class for you because you surely aren't receiving an A! Don't believe me, take this class with her and find out for yourself! One of the WORST professors at UofA and highly recommend taking a different teacher! If your a 4.0 student, Congratulations, you are now a 3.8 student:)

ATTENTION! You will get a B in this class! If you are a 4.0 student think again before taking this class because you surely will not have one after taking this class. Although it is potentially possible to receive an A with this teacher, the overwhelming majority of students taking this course will receive a B. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS with her!

Idk why Aimee has such a low rating. This class was so easy if you just followed the outlines she gave you and paid attention in the lecture you're supposed to attend. She goes over almost every written assignment and shows you exactly how it should look and what to write. This class was not hard.

One of the worst teachers. This class is so easy and helpful with Amber or any other teacher. VERY harsh grader but doesn't give feedback, is inconsistent and disrespectful. Hard to stay awake in class. Not helpful when you ask a question she just says "why don't you tell me" at anything. Takes long to grade. DO NOT TAKE!!!!!!

She is one of the worst professors ive ever had at the UofA. Highly recommended not taking her class and finding a different teacher. She is a very poor communicator and does not treat students well. She is very unprofessional and said that it was frustrating to have to answer my email.

Professor McFarland is a great teacher. Her lectures prepare you for assignments, as she gives examples of them, so attend class. Take advantage of fishbowls as she will tell you what to correct. If you aren't sure about anything reach out to her or attend office hours. She is so helpful and wants you to succeed. Follow the rubrics for good grades!

Aimee is a great teacher and incredibly understanding. Her work is laid out so easily and as long as you follow directions you will get the grade you want. I don't know how she can make her class any easier. Make sure you do what she asks and you will get an A. Definitely recommend!

Aimee is a good professor, she does a good job of explaining the content. However, to achieve a good grade in this class, you are going to have to do way more work than people who take BCOM with another professor. No extra credit opps and a strict class setting. BCOM is already a mindnumbing class, at least take it with a fun professor.

She is a solid professor. Nice lady, very willing to help students. My biggest issue was that her assignment criteria was kind of unclear and she was a tough grader and kind of nit-picky. Other than that I recommend her for this course.

Aimee was one of the best professors I've had at the U of A! Attendance is mandatory but you are able to miss a certain amount of classes without penalty. 

I  HIGHLY recommend attending office hours to have her review your work and guarantee yourself an A.

She is so amazing!! I took her last semester for BCOM 214, and even though I changed my major from business I still did not drop her class because she is the most understanding and helpful professor I have had at the University of Arizona. She always gives great feedback and is extremely understanding. Love her!!

I took Aimee's class a year ago and had the chance to use what I learned during my summer internship. Its easy to get an A in her class if you do what your supposed to and read feedback. I don't understand the negative ratings except that she expects students to learn in her class. I pay for school myself so I'm glad she made it worth my money.

Aimee is amazing, I really felt her course content was relevant and she was right there anytime anyone had questions. I always knew exactly what to expect from the assignments and exams. I wish she taught all my classes. :)

Aimee is the most empathetic professor I have ever had, she cares so much about her students and wants them to succeed. You will do well in her class if you attend class as much as you can and do your work! I highly recommend taking her course.

Great professor. Compared my friends BCOM professors and they had more steps to assignments while we did not! I am glad I took her class because she is such a caring person and will always look out for you!

I don't understand the bad reviews for Aimee she is such a caring person and will always be there to help with either personal or academically situations. Her office hours were super useful. The assignments weren't tough because in class she would go over it. For exams, the study guides were helpful because it'd give you a good grasp on the topics.

Puts students and their mental health first; very approachable and makes herself available. Just come to most classes and you will know how to get an A on every assignment, and if you are confused she's always willing to help or review your work with you via email or during office hours.

Aimee is a good person. I don't think she realizes how means she's being, but she also means well. BCOM214 is a waste of a class, but that isn't her fault. She is a tough grader and will make you work for your grade, but an A is very possible. She is someone who is there for you as a student, which definitely made me feel better.

Aimee is very sweet and her class is very passable if you go to office hours. She is very willing to help and wants you to learn the material. Grading criteria can still be confusing and it's not clear what you lose points on. Try and get things done early and get feedback before you turn it in.

I'll start off by saying I do not understand all the poor ratings for this professor. She is super friendly and approachable and fair with grading. The trick to succeeding in her class is to take good notes on formatting she uses for her writing assignments. I did this every time and got all but one A on an assignment, and I am not a strong writer.

She kind of annoyed me. Her lectures weren't very helpful for assignments- everyone was always really confused on what we were supposed to be doing. The instructions were also super vague. She also took random points away from assignments w/ little to no explanation. Shes nice, just not the best professor in the world

Aimee is a great professor and truly wants her students to succeed. If you pay attention when she fishbowls and points out mistakes, assignments will be very easy for you. There were a lot of assignments but shouldn't take you more than 2 hours. Very understanding. If you're going to miss class let her know and she won't count it as an absence.
Lectures are not very useful in doing projects and lack important content. Do not feel to learn a lot from this class from a lot of homework. Also hard to get a good grade. Boring class overall.

Aimee has been so understanding this semester! She extends most deadlines if you ask and fishbowls a lot of assignments during class. She is very helpful with feedback. There are a lot of assignments, and most of them are easy. Attendance is mandatory, but class is interesting and worthwhile to earn feedback.

Aimee deserves a 5/5. She has been very understanding during covid and has extended deadlines multiple times so we could get extra feedback during class before submitting. There are many assignments but they aren't extremely difficult if you ask questions and some are just note taking assignments-super easy. She makes class enjoyable! Take her

Professor Mcfarland cares a lot about her students and is always willing to extend extra help if you need it. Her assignments require following directions closely and you have to work hard, but if you do you will be successful. I would recommend asking lots of questions and making sure to stay on top of assignments.
please save yourself the pain and take this class with a different professor. She is a terrible teacher

If you want to get an A in BCOM214, don't take this class. If you have a choice, Do Not pick this teacher you will work 10 times as hard for projects ad papers while getting a lower score at every possible point. She says that she is preparing you for the "real" world, well this ain't the real world, this is college. we are trying to learn.

I read the ratings here and got nervous when it came time to choose p/f because of the comments. I didn't think I would do well on the research report. I ended up getting an A and would have gotten an A in the class. Aimee told me I would likely do well, but I didnt believe her. Now I regret it.

Aimee is an incredible woman! She is sweet, caring, & enjoys her job. However, she is a very harsh grader w/ unclear feedback & difficult-to-understand instructions. I really think she'd be a much better counselor or therapist than a BCOM professor. Possible to do work last minute and still get by. I selected the Pass/Fail bc GPA would def tank.

Funny how people who don't do well on assignments vent on Coursicle. McFarland is a tough grader but she gives feedback to tell you what you did wrong and makes herself very available. I want to be prepared for writing and my classes at Eller and that's what she does.

Professor McFarland seems like a great person, but she is a really harsh grader. You really have to stay on your A game the entire semester to even barely get an A. This makes it hard when you're learning new information because it gives you no room to make any mistakes. Definitely go to her office hours though!
Incredibly rude to students and super hard grader for no reason. Passive aggressive towards any questions and gives no guideline on how to do assignments. Take with another professor if possible.

Continuing the trend of substandard ratings, let me add my two cents. What a terrible experience with this Professor. Inconsistent grading and lack of assignment clarity were my main points of frustration. Find an alternative route at Eller

Aimee cares about her students. Not only does she want them to do well in her class, she also wants them to be successful in the real world. She's a tough grader, but think of it as tough love. Whether it's reading extra material or seeking for help during office hours, this class requires students to actively participate and put in the effort.

Aimee is a teacher that cares about her subject matter and holds her students to a high standard. Attendance is mandatory and due dates are rigid. As other posts have mentioned, understanding what is expected in a given assignment was tough at times. Visit her office hours and make sure she knows you and you'll be just fine in the class.

The worst teacher at Eller. She sucks. Avoid this professor at all costs. I was forced to take it with her. She is the toughest grader ever. Her lectures were of no help. She is mean to all of her students. You do all of these pointless assignments that you will never use in the real world.
I was afraid of taking this class but I have learned so much. I am doing an internship over the summer and feel confident that I will be able to communicate effectively.
Definitely take her. I learned so much.

Definitely knows her stuff. I thought she was a harsh grader, although she did give clear grading criteria for every assignment. After getting through my first semester at Eller, though, I realize why she had the expectations she did. I was prepared and did very well. Thanks for getting me ready for Eller!

McFarland was definitely tough, but she got me prepared for Eller and the professional world. A boss would expect me to perform well, and she is no different. While it was tough throughout the semester, I know I learned a lot.

It ain't high school no more. Read instructions! Turn in work on time! Go to office hours! Ask Q's if unclear on an asgmt! Take responsibility for the grades received. If u can't put in effort now, u won't even be able to experience how challenging Eller gets (bc u have to pass class first, u know). She's tough, but that just means work harder.

Aimee's class/assignments are difficult at times (her standards are high), but she is willing to work with students and meet outside of class. Meeting with her in office hours helps tremendously because she explains things; however, it's up to you to put in the work, effort, and show up to class.
Assignments are graded tough, be sure to follow all directions. Overall, the class was fine.

You have to take BCOM 214 and from what I've heard, Paradis is probably the best professor to take it with. Regardless, you have to put in the work in this class. 1 assignment a week on avg. She's nice and emails back quickly. Glad I had her compared to the stories I've heard about the other professors. Paradis takes attendance everyday too so :(
touch on grading,but nice teacher

Erin was an amazing professor and made each class fun. The assignment are not that hard and the tests are easy if you regularly attend class. Erin gives great feedback and I looked forward to going to this class each week. I would 10/10 recommend taking BCOM with Erin.
